frontmatter.title: Professional Cloud Architect Certification Exam Guide

children.value: A Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect enables organizations to leverage Google Cloud technologies. Through an understanding of cloud architecture and Google technology, this individual designs, develops, and manages robust, secure, scalable, highly available, and dynamic solutions to drive business objectives.

The Cloud Architect should be proficient in all aspects of enterprise cloud strategy, solution design, and architectural best practices. The Cloud Architect should also be experienced in software development methodologies and approaches including multi-tiered distributed applications which span multicloud or hybrid environments.

children.label: Case Studies

children.value: During the exam for the Cloud Architect Certification, some of the questions may refer you to a case study that describes a fictitious business and solution concept. These case studies are intended to provide additional context to help you choose your answers. Review the case studies that may be used in the exam:
Section 1: Designing and Planning a Cloud Solution Architecture (~24% of the exam)

children.label: Section 1: Designing and Planning a Cloud Solution Architecture (~24% of the exam)

children.value: 1.1 Designing a Solution Infrastructure that Meets Business Requirements

children.label: 1.1 Designing a Solution Infrastructure that Meets Business Requirements

children.value: Business use cases and product strategy
Success measurements (e.g., key performance indicators [KPI], return on investment [ROI], metrics)
1.2 Designing a Solution Infrastructure that Meets Technical Requirements

children.label: 1.2 Designing a Solution Infrastructure that Meets Technical Requirements

children.value: Elasticity of cloud resources with respect to quotas and limits
1.3 Designing Network, Storage, and Compute Resources

children.label: 1.3 Designing Network, Storage, and Compute Resources

children.value: Cloud-native networking (VPC, peering, firewalls, container networking)
Choosing appropriate storage types (e.g., object, file, databases)
Choosing compute resources (e.g., spot, custom machine type, specialized workload)
Mapping compute needs to platform products
1.4 Creating a Migration Plan (i.e., Documents and Architectural Diagrams)

children.label: 1.4 Creating a Migration Plan (i.e., Documents and Architectural Diagrams)

children.value: Migrating systems and data to support the solution

children.label: 1.5 Envisioning Future Solution Improvements

children.value: Section 2: Managing and Provisioning a Solution Infrastructure (~15% of the exam)

children.label: Section 2: Managing and Provisioning a Solution Infrastructure (~15% of the exam)
2.1 Configuring Network Topologies

children.value: Extending to a multicloud environment that may include Google Cloud to Google Cloud communication
Security protection (e.g., intrusion protection, access control, firewalls)

children.label: 2.2 Configuring Individual Storage Systems

children.value: Network configuration for data transfer and latency
Data retention and data life cycle management

children.label: 2.3 Configuring Compute Systems

children.value: Network configuration for compute resources (Google Compute Engine, Google Kubernetes Engine, serverless networking)
Infrastructure orchestration, resource configuration, and patch management
Section 3: Designing for Security and Compliance (~18% of the exam)

children.label: Section 3: Designing for Security and Compliance (~18% of the exam)
3.1 Designing for Security

children.value: Data security (key management, encryption, secret management)
Security controls (e.g., auditing, VPC Service Controls, context aware access, organization policy)
Managing customer-managed encryption keys with Cloud Key Management Service

children.label: 3.2 Designing for Compliance

children.value: Legislation (e.g., health record privacy, children’s privacy, data privacy, and ownership)
Commercial (e.g., sensitive data such as credit card information handling, personally identifiable information [PII])
Section 4: Analyzing and Optimizing Technical and Business Processes (~18% of the exam)

children.label: Section 4: Analyzing and Optimizing Technical and Business Processes (~18% of the exam)
4.1 Analyzing and Defining Technical Processes

children.value: Troubleshooting / root cause analysis best practices
Testing and validation of software and infrastructure

children.label: 4.2 Analyzing and Defining Business Processes

children.value: 4.3 Developing Procedures to Ensure Reliability of Solutions in Production

children.label: 4.3 Developing Procedures to Ensure Reliability of Solutions in Production

children.value: Section 5: Managing Implementation (~11% of the exam)

children.label: Section 5: Managing Implementation (~11% of the exam)

children.value: 5.1 Advising Development/Operation Teams to Ensure Successful Deployment of the Solution

children.label: 5.1 Advising Development/Operation Teams to Ensure Successful Deployment of the Solution

children.value: Data and system migration and management tooling

children.label: 5.2 Interacting with Google Cloud Programmatically

children.value: Google Cloud SDK (gcloud, gsutil and bq)
Cloud Emulators (e.g., Bigtable, Spanner, Pub/Sub, Firestore)
Section 6: Ensuring Solution and Operations Reliability (~14% of the exam)

children.label: Section 6: Ensuring Solution and Operations Reliability (~14% of the exam)

children.value: Assisting with the support of deployed solutions

children.label: Example Exam Questions

children.value: Section 1: Designing and Planning a Cloud Solution Architecture

children.label: Section 1: Designing and Planning a Cloud Solution Architecture

children.value: What key factors should be considered when designing a cloud solution to meet specific business objectives and KPIs?
How would you ensure high availability and scalability when architecting a solution for a rapidly growing online service?
What steps are involved in creating a migration plan for moving an on-premises application to Google Cloud?
Section 2: Managing and Provisioning a Solution Infrastructure

children.label: Section 2: Managing and Provisioning a Solution Infrastructure

children.value: Which Google Cloud networking features enable secure hybrid connectivity between on-premises and cloud environments?
How do you choose the appropriate storage type for a workload with unpredictable data growth and strict latency requirements?
What considerations should be made when provisioning compute resources for a containerized application on Google Kubernetes Engine?
Section 3: Designing for Security and Compliance

children.label: Section 3: Designing for Security and Compliance

children.value: How can you implement identity and access management (IAM) to enforce least privilege across multiple projects?
What Google Cloud services can be used to encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit?
How would you design a solution to meet regulatory requirements for storing healthcare data in Google Cloud?
Section 4: Analyzing and Optimizing Technical and Business Processes

children.label: Section 4: Analyzing and Optimizing Technical and Business Processes

children.value: What best practices should be followed for troubleshooting and root cause analysis in a cloud environment?
How can cost optimization be achieved when managing cloud resources for a large enterprise?
What procedures can be implemented to ensure business continuity and disaster recovery for mission-critical applications?

children.label: Section 5: Managing Implementation

children.value: What strategies can development teams use to ensure successful deployment and rollback of cloud applications?
Which Google Cloud SDK tools are essential for automating infrastructure provisioning and management?
How would you integrate load and integration testing into the deployment pipeline for a cloud-native application?
Section 6: Ensuring Solution and Operations Reliability

children.label: Section 6: Ensuring Solution and Operations Reliability

children.value: What Google Cloud services can be used to monitor, log, and alert on application performance issues?
How can deployment and release management be automated to minimize downtime and risk?
What measures should be taken to evaluate and maintain the reliability of deployed cloud solutions?
